subtasks.description: Query sam_licenses collection to extract Adobe product lines with current contract terms (product, quantity, unit cost, contract end date, status) grouped by product line

Query consumption_facts for Q4 2024 actual Adobe spend and 90-day usage trend by product line. Generate aggregated metrics: total Q4 spend, average daily usage last 90 days, trend slope (accel/decel), utilization rate vs contracted quantity

Reconcile current contracted state (mongo_adobe_contracts) with Q4 spend and 90-day usage trend (sql_adobe_spend_usage). For each product line, classify renewal posture (expand/hold/reduce), calculate cost efficiency, identify over/under-utilization. Generate renewal trajectory and expansion/reduction recommendations with rationale

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks, minimum required for complete analysis: (1) mongo_query to fetch current contract state from sam_licenses (small tier—straightforward collection lookup by vendor); (2) sql_gen to compute spend aggregates and 90-day trends from consumption_facts (mid tier—multiple aggregation metrics, time-window calculations); (3) cross_recon at frontier tier to reconcile both sources, assess utilization gaps, and generate posture classification + recommendations with multi-signal logic (trend acceleration, cost efficiency, contractual vs. actual consumption deltas). Classification and recommendation generation are integral to the reconciliation step, not separate tasks. Frontier tier needed for sophisticated posture decision-making that weighs financial, operational, and trend signals holistically.

output.sql: WITH q4_2024_spend AS (SELECT cf.publisher, cf.product, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_q4_spend, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.event_date) AS days_with_activity FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher = 'Adobe' AND cf.event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND cf.event_date <= '2024-12-31' AND cf.fiscal_quarter = 4 AND cf.fiscal_year = 2024 GROUP BY cf.publisher, cf.product), last_90_days_usage AS (SELECT cf.publisher, cf.product, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_usage, MAX(cf.daily_active_seats) AS peak_daily_usage, MIN(cf.daily_active_seats) AS min_daily_usage, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.event_date) AS usage_days FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher = 'Adobe' AND cf.event_date >= (CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days') GROUP BY cf.publisher, cf.product), usage_trend AS (SELECT cf.publisher, cf.product, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (MAX(cf.event_date) - MIN(cf.event_date))) / 86400.0 AS days_span, (REGR_SLOPE(cf.daily_active_seats, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM cf.event_date)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.product)) AS trend_slope_raw FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher = 'Adobe' AND cf.event_date >= (CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days') GROUP BY cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.event_date, cf.daily_active_seats, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM cf.event_date)) SELECT q4.publisher, q4.product, q4.total_q4_spend, q4.days_with_activity, l90.avg_daily_usage, l90.peak_daily_usage, l90.min_daily_usage, l90.usage_days, CASE WHEN ut.trend_slope_raw > 0 THEN 'ACCELERATION' WHEN ut.trend_slope_raw < 0 THEN 'DECELERATION' ELSE 'STABLE' END AS usage_trend_direction, ROUND(CAST(ut.trend_slope_raw AS NUMERIC), 4) AS trend_slope_seats_per_day, ROUND(CAST(l90.avg_daily_usage / NULLIF(l90.peak_daily_usage, 0) * 100 AS NUMERIC), 2) AS utilization_vs_peak_pct FROM q4_2024_spend q4 LEFT JOIN last_90_days_usage l90 ON q4.publisher = l90.publisher AND q4.product = l90.product LEFT JOIN usage_trend ut ON q4.publisher = ut.publisher AND q4.product = ut.product ORDER BY q4.total_q4_spend DESC
